---
title: 'Darrell&#39;s Tossed Together Peanut Butter - Curry'
date: '2012-02-08T22:59:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'LIFE in Review (s)'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '5030238019001825696'
blogger_filename: '/2012/02/darrell-tossed-together-peanut-butter.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/LIFE in Review (s)/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Enjoy'
---

<br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">I have learned that Curry is a random name for a bunch of different stuff thrown together to make a tasty sauce. For a brief description see&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry" target="_blank">Wikipedia</a>:</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><em><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry" target="_blank"><strong>Curry</strong>&nbsp;</a>(<img alt="play" data-mce-src="http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Loudspeaker.svg/11px-Loudspeaker.svg.png" src="http://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8a/Loudspeaker.svg/11px-Loudspeaker.svg.png" height="11" style="border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: initial; border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: initial; border-top-width: 0px;" width="11" />&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">/</a><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">ˈ</a><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">k</a><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">ʌr</a><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English#Key" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">i</a><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:IPA_for_English" title="Wikipedia:IPA for English">/</a>) is a generic description used throughout Western culture to describe a variety of dishes from&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Indian_cuisine" title="Indian cuisine">Indian</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistani_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pakistani_cuisine" title="Pakistani cuisine">Pakistani</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangladeshi_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bangladeshi_cuisine" title="Bangladeshi cuisine">Bangladeshi</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sri_Lankan_cuisine" title="Sri Lankan cuisine">Sri Lankan</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thai_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thai_cuisine" title="Thai cuisine">Thai</a>&nbsp;or other&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southeast_Asian_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Southeast_Asian_cuisine" title="Southeast Asian cuisine">Southeast Asian cuisines</a>. The chief spices found in most&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Asia" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/South_Asia" title="South Asia">South Asian</a>&nbsp;curry powders are&nbsp;<strong><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric" title="Turmeric">turmeric</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coriander" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coriander" title="Coriander">coriander</a>, and&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cumin" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cumin" title="Cumin">cumin</a></strong>; a wide range of additional spices may be included depending on the geographic region and the foods being included (white/red meat, fish, lentils, rice and vegetables).<sup><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry#cite_note-0" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curry#cite_note-0">[1]</a></sup></em></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><em>Curry's popularity in recent decades has spread outward from Southern Asia to figure prominently in international cooking. Consequently, each culture has adopted spices in its indigenous cooking to suit its own unique tastes and cultural sensibilities. Curry can therefore be called a&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pan-Asian" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pan-Asian" title="Pan-Asian">pan-Asian</a>&nbsp;or&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Globalization" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Globalization" title="Globalization">global</a>&nbsp;phenomenon with immense popularity in Thai,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/British_cuisine" title="British cuisine">British</a>,&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japanese_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japanese_cuisine" title="Japanese cuisine">Japanese</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caribbean_cuisine" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caribbean_cuisine" title="Caribbean cuisine">Caribbean</a>&nbsp;cuisines.</em></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">I prefer above all else Thai Curry's. Specifically Red Curry with bamboo shoots. However, I have yet to get the process for making a good Thai curry anywhere close to the restaurants so I don't even try these days. I just go to a&nbsp;Thai&nbsp;restaurant.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">Locally, if you live in the DFW area, a very decent set of Thai Curry dishes is produced by:&nbsp;<a data-mce-href="http://maps.google.com/maps/place?hl=en&amp;rlz=1C1GPCK_enUS434US434&amp;nord=1&amp;gs_upl=&amp;bav=on.2,or.r_gc.r_pw.r_cp.,cf.osb&amp;ion=1&amp;biw=1298&amp;bih=683&amp;um=1&amp;ie=UTF-8&amp;q=thai+keller&amp;fb=1&amp;gl=us&amp;hq=thai+keller&amp;cid=4344287535141297151" href="http://maps.google.com/maps/place?hl=en&amp;rlz=1C1GPCK_enUS434US434&amp;nord=1&amp;gs_upl=&amp;bav=on.2,or.r_gc.r_pw.r_cp.,cf.osb&amp;ion=1&amp;biw=1298&amp;bih=683&amp;um=1&amp;ie=UTF-8&amp;q=thai+keller&amp;fb=1&amp;gl=us&amp;hq=thai+keller&amp;cid=4344287535141297151" target="_blank">Sea Siam in Keller Texas</a>a</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;">My favorite Curry Recipe to throw together is a thicker peanut style.</div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"> <dl><dt><a data-mce-href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030530.jpg" href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030530.jpg"><img alt="" data-mce-src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030530.jpg" src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030530.jpg" height="283" style="border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: initial; border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: initial; border-top-width: 0px;" title="P1030530" width="378" /></a></dt><dd>This one has rice, black beans, red kidney beans, ground turkey, and sauce. Simple</dd></dl></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>Ingredients:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>Favorite Meat (Ground Beef, Ground Turkey,&nbsp;Cubed&nbsp;Chicken Breasts, and/or other meats)</li><li>Favorite Veggies (Whatever you have around the house. My favorite are things like Corn, Celery, Bell Peppers, Carrots... whatever you want, sometimes less if more)</li><li>Favorite Starches/Beans (Rice cooked slightly al dente', Red Kidney Beans, Black Beans, Whatever you want.</li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>For the Sauce:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>Butter</li><li>1/2 Onion</li><li>Ketchup</li><li>Peanut Butter</li><li>Milk</li><li>Turmeric</li><li>Cinnamon</li><li>Basil</li><li>Lime (lime juice)(Lemon works too if you don't have lime)</li><li>Salt/Pepper</li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>Also Optional are:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>Pumpkin Spice Seasoning</li><li>Cumin</li><li>Heat (by adding Cajun Seasoning, or Chili Powder, or Crushed red pepper, or other hot pepper spice or hot sauce use sparingly and to taste.)</li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong><a data-mce-href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030529.jpg" href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030529.jpg"><img alt="" data-mce-src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030529.jpg?w=150" src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030529.jpg?w=150" height="112" style="border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: initial; border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: initial; border-top-width: 0px;" title="P1030529" width="150" /></a>Instructions:</strong></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>To Cook the Curry Sauce Itself:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>Melt 2-3 Tablespoons of&nbsp;<strong>butter</strong></li><li>Saute' in the butter some chopped&nbsp;<strong>onions</strong>&nbsp;(More or less than a 1/2 an onion your choice I like onions!)</li><li>Optional affects the taste profile. (Depending on how I feel that day I may also add some fresh chopped&nbsp;<strong>garlic</strong>&nbsp;cloves with the onions)</li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>Once saute'd for a few moments add:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>Good squeeze of&nbsp;<strong>Ketchup</strong>&nbsp;(which is really just sugar and tomato paste)(Less than a quarter cup)</li><li>Two-Four heaping spoons of&nbsp;<strong>Peanut Butter</strong>&nbsp;(Crunchy works best)</li><li>1/2 to 1 cup of&nbsp;<strong>Milk</strong>&nbsp;(I prefer vanilla soy milk, it adds a sweetness to the sauce)</li><li>3 or 4 &nbsp;heavy dashes of&nbsp;<strong><em><a data-mce-href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric" href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Turmeric" title="Turmeric">Turmeric</a></em></strong></li><li>4-5 heavy dashes of&nbsp;<strong>Cinnamon&nbsp;</strong>(Substitute Cinnamon with Pumpkin Spice Seasoning for more complexity)</li><li>1-2 Dashes of&nbsp;<strong>Basil</strong></li><li>Squeeze of concentrated&nbsp;<strong>lime juice</strong>&nbsp;or half a lime.</li><li>Little bit of&nbsp;<strong>salt</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>pepper</strong></li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong><a data-mce-href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030532.jpg" href="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030532.jpg"><img alt="" data-mce-src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030532.jpg?w=150" src="http://kogconcepts.files.wordpress.com/2011/10/p1030532.jpg?w=150" height="112" style="border-bottom-width: 0px; border-color: initial; border-image: initial; border-left-width: 0px; border-right-width: 0px; border-style: initial; border-top-width: 0px;" title="P1030532" width="150" /></a>Also Optional:</strong></div><ul style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><li>2-3&nbsp;dashes&nbsp;of&nbsp;<strong>Cumin&nbsp;</strong>(optional but adds to complexity)</li><li>Heat ( Cajun Spice Seasoning)(Or favorite hot pepper spice)</li><li>Pumpkin&nbsp;Spice Seasoning</li></ul><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong><em><a data-mce-href="http://busycooks.about.com/library/recipes/blspicemix.htm" href="http://busycooks.about.com/library/recipes/blspicemix.htm" target="_blank">Pumpkin Spice Seasoning has</a></em></strong></div><div id="abm" style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><div id="abc"><div id="articlebody"><ul><li><em>1 Tbsp. cinnamon</em></li><li><em>1-1/2 tsp. ground ginger</em></li><li><em>1/2 tsp. ground cloves</em></li><li><em>1/2 tsp. ground nutmeg</em></li><li><em>1/8 tsp. salt</em></li></ul></div><div id="articlebody"><em>If you don't have pumpkin spice seasoning just add some&nbsp;<strong>Cinnamon and Turmeric</strong>... all other spices just add to the complexity and make it better but are not essential for the mix.&nbsp;</em></div></div><div>The sauce should be the consistency of a runny, maybe even chunky, pancake batter. Don't cook or simmer too long or the peanut butter will separate into it's natural oils and ruin the whole batch.</div><div>If choosing to serve all combined in a bowl it will more thick not like a soup. Still tasty!</div><div><strong>Once ingredients heat to a good steam and bubble remove from heat immediately.&nbsp;</strong></div><div>After getting the sauce ready you can add the sauce on top of Rice, Beans (Black, Red Kidney, and/or Pinto) and Ground Turky or Beef browned, and or Chicken cubed and saute'd. Also feel free to cook your favorite veggies I like Bell Peppers, Carrots, Corn removed from&nbsp;Cobb, Celery, Etc... More or less is all up to you. Try different combinations, or deal with what you have. This is really a toss together meal.</div></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>You could also add all these to the curry itself for a more soup like presentation, great with a good Dr. Pepper and garlic toast and a good movie, like Iron Man or Stargate SG1!&nbsp;</strong></div><div style="font-family: Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Bitstream Charter', Times, serif; font-size: 13px; line-height: 19px;"><strong>Darrell G. Wolfe</strong></div>
